items.headline: Florida center Rueben Chinyelu to test NBA draft waters
items.description: Florida center Rueben Chinyelu, ranked No. 57 on ESPN's big board, is entering the NBA draft while maintaining his college eligibility.

items.headline: Carlos Alcaraz waits on wrist tests as French Open awaits
items.description: Defending two-time champion Carlos Alcaraz has put his participation at the French Open in doubt because of a right wrist injury, and medical tests in the next few days will determine whether Alcaraz will be able to play in the Grand Slam tournament.

items.headline: Storm's Ezi Magbegor to miss start of season with foot injury
items.description: Storm standout Ezi Magbegor will miss the beginning of the WNBA season due to a right foot injury that she suffered while playing for Australian national team.

items.headline: Vissel Kobe fall short again as Al Ahli close in on AFC Champions League Elite title defence
items.description: Al Ahli are through to a second consecutive AFC Champions League Elite final after coming from behind to beat Vissel Kobe 2-1.

items.headline: Sources: Oilers expect Leon Draisaitl to return for Game 1
items.description: Edmonton Oilers star center Leon Draisaitl, who has not played since March 15 after suffering a lower-body injury, is expected to return for Game 1 of the Stanley Cup playoffs against the Anaheim Ducks on Monday night, sources told ESPN's Emily Kaplan.

items.headline: Raw After WrestleMania: Top moments in WWE history
items.description: Raw After WrestleMania has become a tradition following WWE's biggest event of the year. Find out more about top moments in history.

items.headline: Jefferson blunt about McCarthy's future with Murray a Viking
items.description: Speaking publicly Monday for the first time about the Vikings' new quarterback room, Justin Jefferson said he is looking forward to Kyler Murray's playmaking skills and was blunt about J.J. McCarthy's future.

items.headline: Real Madrid beat Brugge to win UEFA Youth League final
items.description: Real Madrid has earned at least one trophy this season and become a European champion again on Monday.

items.headline: Draymond Green predicts Steve Kerr won't be back next season
items.description: Draymond Green said while he hopes Steve Kerr is back, he'd guess that this past season was Kerr's last as coach with the franchise.

items.headline: What are the longest losing streaks in MLB history?
items.description: The 2024 Chicago White Sox own the most recent addition to the longest MLB losing streaks, at 21 games. Which other teams make the list?

items.headline: Tilak Varma rides 45-ball rollercoaster to IPL glory
items.description: From 19 off 22 balls to equalling the record for MI's fastest century, this was one wild ride

items.headline: Hardik: 'Tilak is so talented that he doesn't have to worry about a lot'
items.description: "It was much needed for the group, it was much needed for Tilak, it was much needed for Mumbai Indians"

items.headline: Stats - Tilak outscores GT, Bumrah ends his drought
items.description: GT's defeat, meanwhile, was their biggest by a runs margin in the IPL

items.headline: Ceddanne Rafaela's tie-breaking two-run single helps Red Sox to 8-6 Patriots' Day win over Tigers
items.description: — Pinch hitter Ceddanne Rafaela had a tiebreaking, two-run infield single in the seventh inning and the Red Sox beat the Detroit Tigers 8-6 on Monday in Boston’s annual Patriots’ Day game to salvage a split of their four-game series.

items.headline: Tilak ends MI's losing streak with 45-ball century
items.description: It was the joint-quickest hundred by an MI batter, equalling Sanath Jayasuriya's effort from 2008
